etymology.description: 初(初) (chū, "beginning; first") + 恋(戀) (liàn, "love; long for")
etymology.interpretation: 初(初) (chū, "beginning; first") indicates the earliest or first instance of something, establishing the context of 'firstness'. 恋(戀) (liàn, "love; long for") expresses the emotion of deep affection or desire. Together, 初戀 (chūliàn, "first love") suggests the initial experience of love or romantic feelings in a person's life.

examples.chinese: 他的初恋(他的初戀)是一位邻居家的女孩(是一位鄰居家的女孩)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de chūliàn shì yī wèi línjū jiā de nǚhái.
examples.english: His first love was a girl from the neighbor's house.

examples.chinese: 初恋的回忆(初戀的回憶)总是最甜美(總是最甜美)。
examples.pinyin: Chūliàn de huíyì zǒng shì zuì tiánměi.
examples.english: The memories of first love are always the sweetest.

examples.chinese: 我在十五岁时(我在十五歲時)遇到我的初恋(遇到我的初戀)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zài shíwǔ suì shí yùdào wǒ de chūliàn.
examples.english: I met my first love when I was fifteen.
